```text
     1→'use client'

     3→import type { CommentModel } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     4→import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'

     7→import { Avatar } from '~/components/ui/avatar'

     9→
    10→import { CommentBoxRoot } from './CommentBox/Root'
    11→import type { BlockAnchor, CommentAnchor } from './types'
    12→
    13→type CommentWithAnchor = CommentModel & { anchor?: CommentAnchor }
    14→
    15→interface CommentBlockThreadProps {
    16→  comments: CommentWithAnchor[]
    17→  refId: string
    18→  blockAnchor: BlockAnchor
    19→  onClose?: () => void
    20→}
    21→
    22→function ThreadComment({ comment }: { comment: CommentWithAnchor }) {
    23→  const { avatar, author } = comment
    24→
    25→  return (
    26→    <div className="flex gap-2 px-3 py-2">
    27→      <Avatar
    28→        shadow={false}
    29→        imageUrl={avatar}
    30→        text={author}
    31→        size={24}
    32→        radius="full"
    33→        className="shrink-0 rounded-full"
    34→      />
    35→      <div className="min-w-0 flex-1">
    36→        <div className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5">
    37→          <span className="text-[13px] font-semibold text-foreground/90">
    38→            {author}
    39→          </span>
    40→          <span className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
    41→            <RelativeTime date={comment.created} />
    42→          </span>
    43→        </div>
    44→        {comment.anchor?.mode === 'range' && (
    45→          <div className="mt-0.5 border-l-2 border-accent/40 pl-2 text-xs italic text-muted-foreground">
    46→            {comment.anchor.quote}
    47→          </div>
    48→        )}
    49→        <p className="mt-0.5 text-[13px] leading-relaxed text-foreground/80">
    50→          {comment.text}
    51→        </p>
    52→      </div>
    53→    </div>
    54→  )
    55→}
    56→
    57→export function CommentBlockThread({
    58→  comments,
    59→  refId,
    60→  blockAnchor,
    61→  onClose,
    62→}: CommentBlockThreadProps) {
    63→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    64→
    65→  const handleAfterSubmit = useCallback(() => {
    66→    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
    67→      queryKey: ['comments', refId, 'anchors'],
    68→    })
    69→    onClose?.()
    70→  }, [queryClient, refId, onClose])
    71→
    72→  return (
    73→    <div className="w-80 min-w-0 overflow-hidden">
    74→      {comments.length > 0 && (
    75→        <div className="max-h-72 overflow-y-auto py-1">
    76→          {comments.map((comment) => (
    77→            <ThreadComment key={comment.id} comment={comment} />
    78→          ))}
    79→        </div>
    80→      )}
    81→      <div className={comments.length > 0 ? 'border-t border-border' : ''}>
    82→        <CommentBoxRoot
    83→          refId={refId}
    84→          anchor={blockAnchor}
    85→          afterSubmit={handleAfterSubmit}
    86→          className="p-2 [&_textarea]:!min-h-[60px]"
    87→        />
    88→      </div>
    89→    </div>
    90→  )
    91→}
    92→
```

```text
     1→'use client'

     3→import type { CommentModel } from '@mx-space/api-client'

     6→import { useIsMobile } from '~/atoms/hooks/viewport'
     7→import { Avatar } from '~/components/ui/avatar'

    11→import type { BlockInfo } from './anchor-utils'
    12→import { buildBlockAnchorFromIndex } from './anchor-utils'
    13→import { CommentBlockThread } from './CommentBlockThread'
    14→import type { BlockAnchor, CommentAnchor } from './types'
    15→
    16→type CommentWithAnchor = CommentModel & { anchor?: CommentAnchor }
    17→
    18→interface CommentBlockGutterProps {
    19→  containerRef: React.RefObject<HTMLElement | null>
    20→  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
    21→  comments: CommentWithAnchor[]
    22→  refId: string
    23→}
    24→
    25→function AvatarStack({
    26→  comments,
    27→  size = 22,
    28→}: {
    29→  comments: CommentWithAnchor[]
    30→  size?: number
    31→}) {
    32→  const uniqueAvatars = useMemo(() => {
    33→    const seen = new Set<string>()
    34→    const result: { avatar?: string; author: string }[] = []
    35→    for (const c of comments) {
    36→      const key = c.avatar || c.author
    37→      if (!seen.has(key)) {
    38→        seen.add(key)
    39→        result.push({ avatar: c.avatar, author: c.author })
    40→      }
    41→    }
    42→    return result.slice(0, 3)
    43→  }, [comments])
    44→
    45→  return (
    46→    <div className="flex items-center -space-x-1.5">
    47→      {uniqueAvatars.map((u) => (
    48→        <Avatar
    49→          key={u.avatar || u.author}
    50→          shadow={false}
    51→          imageUrl={u.avatar}
    52→          text={u.author}
    53→          size={size}
    54→          radius="full"
    55→          className="rounded-full ring-2 ring-white dark:ring-zinc-900"
    56→        />
    57→      ))}
    58→    </div>
    59→  )
    60→}
    61→
    62→function DesktopGutterItem({
    63→  blockIndex,
    64→  blockComments,
    65→  blockInfos,
    66→  refId,
    67→  top,
    68→  isHovered,
    69→  onOpen,
    70→  onClose,
    71→}: {
    72→  blockIndex: number
    73→  blockComments: CommentWithAnchor[]
    74→  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
    75→  refId: string
    76→  top: number
    77→  isHovered: boolean
    78→  onOpen: () => void
    79→  onClose: () => void
    80→}) {
    81→  const hasComments = blockComments.length > 0
    82→  const anchor = buildBlockAnchorFromIndex(blockInfos, blockIndex)
    83→
    84→  if (!anchor) return null
    85→
    86→  const triggerEl = hasComments ? (
    87→    <button
    88→      type="button"
    89→      className="flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-1 rounded-full py-0.5 pl-0.5 pr-1.5 transition-colors hover:bg-zinc-100 dark:hover:bg-zinc-800"
    90→    >
    91→      <AvatarStack comments={blockComments} />
    92→      <span className="ml-0.5 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground">
    93→        {blockComments.length}
    94→      </span>
    95→    </button>
    96→  ) : (
    97→    <button
    98→      type="button"
    99→      className={`flex size-7 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center rounded-md text-muted-foreground transition-all hover:bg-zinc-100 hover:text-foreground dark:hover:bg-zinc-800 ${
   100→        isHovered ? 'opacity-100' : 'opacity-0'
   101→      }`}
   102→    >
   103→      <i className="icon-[mingcute--comment-plus-line] text-[15px]" />
   104→    </button>
   105→  )
   106→
   107→  return (
   108→    <div
   109→      className="absolute left-0 flex items-start"
   110→      style={{ top: `${top}px` }}
   111→    >
   112→      <FloatPopover
   113→        triggerElement={triggerEl}
   114→        trigger="click"
   115→        placement="right-start"
   116→        offset={8}
   117→        headless
   118→        popoverClassNames="rounded-xl border border-zinc-200/80 bg-white shadow-lg overflow-hidden dark:border-zinc-700/80 dark:bg-zinc-900"
   119→        onOpen={onOpen}
   120→        onClose={onClose}
   121→      >
   122→        <CommentBlockThread
   123→          comments={blockComments}
   124→          refId={refId}
   125→          blockAnchor={anchor as BlockAnchor}
   126→        />
   127→      </FloatPopover>
   128→    </div>
   129→  )
   130→}
   131→
   132→function MobileGutterItem({
   133→  blockIndex,
   134→  blockComments,
   135→  blockInfos,
   136→  refId,
   137→  top,
   138→  onOpen,
   139→  onClose,
   140→}: {
   141→  blockIndex: number
   142→  blockComments: CommentWithAnchor[]
   143→  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
   144→  refId: string
   145→  top: number
   146→  onOpen: () => void
   147→  onClose: () => void
   148→}) {
   149→  const anchor = buildBlockAnchorFromIndex(blockInfos, blockIndex)
   150→
   151→  if (!anchor) return null
   152→
   153→  return (
   154→    <div
   155→      className="absolute -right-1 flex items-start"
   156→      style={{ top: `${top}px` }}
   157→    >
   158→      <PresentSheet
   159→        triggerAsChild
   160→        title="评论"
   161→        content={
   162→          <CommentBlockThread
   163→            comments={blockComments}
   164→            refId={refId}
   165→            blockAnchor={anchor as BlockAnchor}
   166→          />
   167→        }
   168→        onOpenChange={(open) => {
   169→          if (open) onOpen()
   170→          else onClose()
   171→        }}
   172→      >
   173→        <button
   174→          type="button"
   175→          className="flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-0.5 rounded-full border border-zinc-200/80 bg-white/90 py-0.5 pl-0.5 pr-1.5 shadow-sm backdrop-blur-sm dark:border-zinc-700/80 dark:bg-zinc-900/90"
   176→        >
   177→          <AvatarStack comments={blockComments} size={20} />
   178→          <span className="text-[10px] font-medium text-muted-foreground">
   179→            {blockComments.length}
   180→          </span>
   181→        </button>
   182→      </PresentSheet>
   183→    </div>
   184→  )
   185→}
   186→
   187→export function CommentBlockGutter({
   188→  containerRef,
   189→  blockInfos,
   190→  comments,
   191→  refId,
   192→}: CommentBlockGutterProps) {
   193→  const isMobile = useIsMobile()
   194→
   195→  const commentsByBlock = useMemo(() => {
   196→    const map = new Map<string, CommentWithAnchor[]>()
   197→    for (const c of comments) {
   198→      const blockId = c.anchor?.blockId
   199→      if (blockId) {
   200→        const arr = map.get(blockId) || []
   201→        arr.push(c)
   202→        map.set(blockId, arr)
   203→      }
   204→    }
   205→    return map
   206→  }, [comments])
   207→
   208→  const [blockPositions, setBlockPositions] = useState<Map<number, number>>(
   209→    () => new Map(),
   210→  )
   211→
   212→  useEffect(() => {
   213→    const container = containerRef.current
   214→    if (!container) return
   215→    const contentEl = container.querySelector('.rich-content')
   216→    if (!contentEl) return
   217→
   218→    const updatePositions = () => {
   219→      const containerRect = container.getBoundingClientRect()
   220→      const positions = new Map<number, number>()
   221→      for (let i = 0; i < contentEl.children.length; i++) {
   222→        const child = contentEl.children[i] as HTMLElement
   223→        const rect = child.getBoundingClientRect()
   224→        positions.set(i, rect.top - containerRect.top)
   225→      }
   226→      setBlockPositions(new Map(positions))
   227→    }
   228→
   229→    updatePositions()
   230→    const observer = new ResizeObserver(updatePositions)
   231→    observer.observe(contentEl)
   232→
   233→    return () => observer.disconnect()
   234→  }, [containerRef, blockInfos])
   235→
   236→  const [hoverIndex, setHoverIndex] = useState(-1)
   237→
   238→  const handleMouseMove = useCallback(
   239→    (e: MouseEvent) => {
   240→      const container = containerRef.current
   241→      if (!container) return
   242→      const contentEl = container.querySelector('.rich-content')
   243→      if (!contentEl) return
   244→
   245→      for (let i = 0; i < contentEl.children.length; i++) {
   246→        const child = contentEl.children[i] as HTMLElement
   247→        const rect = child.getBoundingClientRect()
   248→        if (e.clientY >= rect.top && e.clientY <= rect.bottom) {
   249→          setHoverIndex(i)
   250→          return
   251→        }
   252→      }
   253→      setHoverIndex(-1)
   254→    },
   255→    [containerRef],
   256→  )
   257→
   258→  const handleMouseLeave = useCallback(() => {
   259→    setHoverIndex(-1)
   260→  }, [])
   261→
   262→  useEffect(() => {
   263→    if (isMobile) return
   264→
   265→    const container = containerRef.current
   266→    if (!container) return
   267→
   268→    container.addEventListener('mousemove', handleMouseMove)
   269→    container.addEventListener('mouseleave', handleMouseLeave)
   270→    return () => {
   271→      container.removeEventListener('mousemove', handleMouseMove)
   272→      container.removeEventListener('mouseleave', handleMouseLeave)
   273→    }
   274→  }, [containerRef, handleMouseMove, handleMouseLeave, isMobile])
   275→
   276→  const [activeBlockId, setActiveBlockId] = useState<string | null>(null)
   277→  const prevActiveRef = useRef<string | null>(null)
   278→
   279→  useEffect(() => {
   280→    const container = containerRef.current
   281→    if (!container) return
   282→    const contentEl = container.querySelector('.rich-content')
   283→    if (!contentEl) return
   284→
   285→    if (prevActiveRef.current) {
   286→      const prevIdx = blockInfos.findIndex(
   287→        (b) => b.blockId === prevActiveRef.current,
   288→      )
   289→      if (prevIdx !== -1) {
   290→        contentEl.children[prevIdx]?.classList.remove('comment-block-active')
   291→      }
   292→    }
   293→
   294→    if (activeBlockId) {
   295→      const idx = blockInfos.findIndex((b) => b.blockId === activeBlockId)
   296→      if (idx !== -1) {
   297→        contentEl.children[idx]?.classList.add('comment-block-active')
   298→      }
   299→    }
   300→
   301→    prevActiveRef.current = activeBlockId
   302→  }, [activeBlockId, containerRef, blockInfos])
   303→
   304→  useEffect(() => {
   305→    const container = containerRef.current
   306→    return () => {
   307→      if (!container) return
   308→      const contentEl = container.querySelector('.rich-content')
   309→      if (!contentEl) return
   310→      for (const child of contentEl.children) {
   311→        child.classList.remove('comment-block-active')
   312→      }
   313→    }
   314→  }, [containerRef])
   315→
   316→  const gutterItems = useMemo(() => {
   317→    const items: {
   318→      blockIndex: number
   319→      blockId: string
   320→      comments: CommentWithAnchor[]
   321→      isGhost: boolean
   322→    }[] = []
   323→
   324→    for (const [blockId, blockComments] of commentsByBlock) {
   325→      const blockIdx = blockInfos.findIndex((b) => b.blockId === blockId)
   326→      if (blockIdx !== -1) {
   327→        items.push({
   328→          blockIndex: blockIdx,
   329→          blockId,
   330→          comments: blockComments,
   331→          isGhost: false,
   332→        })
   333→      }
   334→    }
   335→
   336→    if (!isMobile && hoverIndex !== -1) {
   337→      const info = blockInfos[hoverIndex]
   338→      if (info?.blockId && !commentsByBlock.has(info.blockId)) {
   339→        items.push({
   340→          blockIndex: hoverIndex,
   341→          blockId: info.blockId,
   342→          comments: [],
   343→          isGhost: true,
   344→        })
   345→      }
   346→    }
   347→
   348→    return items
   349→  }, [commentsByBlock, blockInfos, hoverIndex, isMobile])
   350→
   351→  // Mobile: only show blocks with comments, inline at right edge
   352→  if (isMobile) {
   353→    const commentItems = gutterItems.filter((item) => !item.isGhost)
   354→    if (commentItems.length === 0) return null
   355→
   356→    return (
   357→      <div className="absolute inset-0 pointer-events-none">
   358→        {commentItems.map((item) => {
   359→          const top = blockPositions.get(item.blockIndex)
   360→          if (top === undefined) return null
   361→
   362→          return (
   363→            <div key={item.blockId} className="pointer-events-auto">
   364→              <MobileGutterItem
   365→                blockIndex={item.blockIndex}
   366→                blockComments={item.comments}
   367→                blockInfos={blockInfos}
   368→                refId={refId}
   369→                top={top}
   370→                onOpen={() => setActiveBlockId(item.blockId)}
   371→                onClose={() => setActiveBlockId(null)}
   372→              />
   373→            </div>
   374→          )
   375→        })}
   376→      </div>
   377→    )
   378→  }
   379→
   380→  // Desktop: absolute gutter to the right
   381→  return (
   382→    <div className="absolute top-0 bottom-0 right-0 hidden w-8 lg:block z-10">
   383→      {gutterItems.map((item) => {
   384→        const top = blockPositions.get(item.blockIndex)
   385→        if (top === undefined) return null
   386→
   387→        return (
   388→          <DesktopGutterItem
   389→            key={item.blockId}
   390→            blockIndex={item.blockIndex}
   391→            blockComments={item.comments}
   392→            blockInfos={blockInfos}
   393→            refId={refId}
   394→            top={top}
   395→            isHovered={hoverIndex === item.blockIndex}
   396→            onOpen={() => setActiveBlockId(item.blockId)}
   397→            onClose={() => setActiveBlockId(null)}
   398→          />
   399→        )
   400→      })}
   401→    </div>
   402→  )
   403→}
   404→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/Root.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     4→
     5→import { useIsOwnerLogged } from '~/atoms/hooks/owner'
     6→import { useSessionReader } from '~/atoms/hooks/reader'
     7→import { ErrorBoundary } from '~/components/common/ErrorBoundary'
     8→import { AutoResizeHeight } from '~/components/modules/shared/AutoResizeHeight'
     9→import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
    10→
    11→import type { CommentBaseProps } from '../types'
    12→import { CommentBoxAuthedInput } from './AuthedInput'
    13→import { CommentBoxLegacyForm } from './CommentBoxLegacyForm'
    14→import { CommentBoxMode, setCommentMode, useCommentMode } from './hooks'
    15→import { CommentBoxProvider } from './providers'
    16→import { CommentBoxSignedOutContent } from './SignedOutContent'
    17→import { SwitchCommentMode } from './SwitchCommentMode'
    18→
    19→export const CommentBoxRoot: Component<CommentBaseProps> = (props) => {
    20→  const { refId, className, afterSubmit, initialValue, autoFocus, anchor } = props
    21→
    22→  const mode = useCommentMode()
    23→
    24→  const isLogged = useIsOwnerLogged()
    25→
    26→  const sessionReader = useSessionReader()
    27→
    28→  useEffect(() => {
    29→    if (sessionReader) {
    30→      setCommentMode(CommentBoxMode['with-auth'])
    31→    }
    32→  }, [sessionReader])
    33→
    34→  return (
    35→    <ErrorBoundary>
    36→      <CommentBoxProvider
    37→        refId={refId}
    38→        afterSubmit={afterSubmit}
    39→        initialValue={initialValue}
    40→        anchor={anchor}
    41→      >
    42→        <div
    43→          className={clsxm('group relative w-full min-w-0', className)}
    44→          data-hide-print
    45→        >
    46→          <SwitchCommentMode />
    47→
    48→          <div className="relative w-full">
    49→            {isLogged ? (
    50→              <CommentBoxLegacy autoFocus={autoFocus} />
    51→            ) : mode === CommentBoxMode.legacy ? (
    52→              <CommentBoxLegacy autoFocus={autoFocus} />
    53→            ) : (
    54→              <CommentBoxWithAuth autoFocus={autoFocus} />
    55→            )}
    56→          </div>
    57→        </div>
    58→      </CommentBoxProvider>
    59→    </ErrorBoundary>
    60→  )
    61→}
    62→
    63→const CommentBoxLegacy: Component<{ autoFocus?: boolean }> = ({
    64→  autoFocus,
    65→}) => (
    66→  <AutoResizeHeight>
    67→    <CommentBoxLegacyForm autoFocus={autoFocus} />
    68→  </AutoResizeHeight>
    69→)
    70→
    71→const CommentBoxWithAuth: Component<{ autoFocus?: boolean }> = ({
    72→  autoFocus,
    73→}) => {
    74→  const isReaderLogin = !!useSessionReader()
    75→
    76→  return (
    77→    <AutoResizeHeight>
```

```text
    35→
    36→export const FloatPopover = function <T extends {}>(
    37→  props: FloatPopoverProps<T> & {
    38→    mobileAsSheet?: boolean
    39→    sheet?: Partial<Omit<PresentSheetProps, 'content'>>
    40→  },
    41→) {
    42→  const isMobile = useIsMobile()
    43→  if (isMobile && props.mobileAsSheet) {
    44→    const { triggerElement, TriggerComponent, triggerComponentProps } = props
    45→
    46→    const Child = triggerElement
    47→      ? triggerElement
    48→      : TriggerComponent
    49→        ? createElement(TriggerComponent as any, triggerComponentProps)
    50→        : null
    51→
    52→    return (
    53→      <PresentSheet content={props.children} {...props.sheet}>
    54→        {Child}
    55→      </PresentSheet>
    56→    )
    57→  }
    58→  return <RealFloatPopover {...props} />
    59→}
    60→
    61→type FloatPopoverProps<T> = PropsWithChildren<{
    62→  triggerElement?: string | ReactElement<any>
    63→  TriggerComponent?: FC<T>
    64→
    65→  headless?: boolean
    66→  wrapperClassName?: string
    67→  trigger?: 'click' | 'hover' | 'both'
    68→  padding?: number
    69→  offset?: number
    70→  popoverWrapperClassNames?: string
    71→  popoverClassNames?: string
    72→
    73→  triggerComponentProps?: T
    74→  /**
    75→   * 不消失
    76→   */
    77→  debug?: boolean
    78→
    79→  animate?: boolean
    80→
    81→  as?: keyof HTMLElementTagNameMap
    82→
    83→  /**
    84→   * @default popover
    85→   */
    86→  type?: 'tooltip' | 'popover'
    87→  isDisabled?: boolean
    88→
    89→  to?: HTMLElement
    90→
    91→  onOpen?: () => void
    92→  onClose?: () => void
    93→
    94→  asChild?: boolean
    95→}> &
    96→  UseFloatingOptions
    97→
    98→const PopoverActionContext = createContext<{
    99→  close: () => void
   100→}>(null!)
   101→
   102→export const usePopoverAction = () => use(PopoverActionContext)
   103→
   104→const RealFloatPopover = function FloatPopover<T extends {}>(
   105→  props: FloatPopoverProps<T>,
   106→) {
   107→  const {
   108→    headless = false,
   109→    wrapperClassName: wrapperClassNames,
   110→    TriggerComponent,
   111→    triggerElement,
   112→    trigger = 'hover',
   113→    padding,
   114→    offset: offsetValue,
   115→    popoverWrapperClassNames,
   116→    popoverClassNames,
   117→    debug,
   118→    animate = true,
   119→    as: As = 'div',
   120→    type = 'popover',
   121→    triggerComponentProps,
   122→    isDisabled,
   123→    onOpen,
   124→    onClose,
   125→    to,
   126→    asChild,
   127→    ...floatingProps
   128→  } = props
   129→
   130→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false)
   131→  const { x, y, refs, strategy, isPositioned, elements, update } = useFloating({
   132→    middleware: floatingProps.middleware ?? [
   133→      flip({ padding: padding ?? 20 }),
   134→      offset(offsetValue ?? 10),
   135→      shift(),
   136→    ],
   137→
   138→    strategy: floatingProps.strategy,
   139→    placement: floatingProps.placement ?? 'bottom-start',
   140→    whileElementsMounted: floatingProps.whileElementsMounted,
   141→  })
   142→
   143→  useEffect(() => {
   144→    if (open && elements.reference && elements.floating) {
   145→      const cleanup = autoUpdate(elements.reference, elements.floating, update)
   146→      return cleanup
   147→    }
   148→  }, [open, elements, update])
   149→
   150→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
   151→
   152→  useClickAway(containerRef, () => {
   153→    if (trigger == 'click' || trigger == 'both') {
   154→      doPopoverDisappear()
   155→    }
   156→  })
   157→
   158→  const doPopoverDisappear = useCallback(() => {
   159→    if (debug) {
   160→      return
   161→    }
   162→    setOpen(false)
   163→  }, [debug])
   164→
   165→  const doPopoverShow = useEventCallback(() => {
   166→    if (isDisabled) return
   167→    setOpen(true)
   168→  })
   169→
   170→  const handleMouseOut = useCallback(() => {
   171→    doPopoverDisappear()
   172→  }, [doPopoverDisappear])
   173→
   174→  const listener = useMemo(() => {
   175→    const baseListener = {
   176→      // onFocus: doPopoverShow,
   177→      // onBlur: doPopoverDisappear,
   178→    }
   179→    switch (trigger) {
   180→      case 'click': {
   181→        return {
   182→          ...baseListener,
   183→          onClick: doPopoverShow,
   184→        }
   185→      }
   186→      case 'hover': {
   187→        return {
   188→          ...baseListener,
   189→          onMouseOver: doPopoverShow,
   190→          onMouseOut: doPopoverDisappear,
   191→        }
   192→      }
   193→      case 'both': {
   194→        return {
   195→          ...baseListener,
   196→          onClick: doPopoverShow,
   197→          onMouseOver: doPopoverShow,
   198→          onMouseOut: handleMouseOut,
   199→        }
   200→      }
   201→    }
   202→  }, [doPopoverDisappear, doPopoverShow, handleMouseOut, trigger])
   203→
   204→  const Child = triggerElement ? (
   205→    triggerElement
   206→  ) : TriggerComponent ? (
   207→    React.cloneElement(
   208→      createElement(TriggerComponent as any, triggerComponentProps),
   209→
   210→      {
   211→        tabIndex: 0,
   212→      },
   213→    )
   214→  ) : (
   215→    <></>
   216→  )
   217→  const TriggerWrapper = asChild ? (
   218→    React.cloneElement(
   219→      typeof Child === 'string' ? <span>{Child}</span> : Child,
   220→      {
   221→        ...listener,
   222→        ref: refs.setReference,
   223→      },
   224→    )
   225→  ) : (
   226→    <As
   227→      // @ts-ignore
   228→      role={trigger === 'both' || trigger === 'click' ? 'button' : 'note'}
   229→      className={clsxm('inline-block', wrapperClassNames)}
   230→      ref={refs.setReference}
   231→      {...listener}
   232→    >
   233→      {Child}
   234→    </As>
   235→  )
   236→
   237→  useEffect(() => {
   238→    if (refs.floating.current && open && type === 'popover') {
   239→      refs.floating.current.focus()
   240→    }
   241→  }, [open])
   242→
   243→  useEffect(() => {
   244→    if (open) {
   245→      onOpen?.()
   246→    } else {
   247→      onClose?.()
   248→    }
   249→  }, [open])
   250→  const actionCtxValue = useMemo(
   251→    () => ({ close: doPopoverDisappear }),
   252→    [doPopoverDisappear],
   253→  )
   254→
   255→  if (!props.children) {
   256→    return TriggerWrapper
   257→  }
   258→
   259→  return (
   260→    <>
   261→      {TriggerWrapper}
   262→
   263→      <AnimatePresence>
   264→        {open && (
   265→          <RootPortal to={to}>
   266→            <m.div
   267→              onWheel={stopPropagation}
   268→              className={clsxm(
   269→                'float-popover',
   270→                'pointer-events-auto relative z-[99]',
   271→                popoverWrapperClassNames,
   272→              )}
   273→              {...(trigger === 'hover' || trigger === 'both' ? listener : {})}
   274→              ref={containerRef}
   275→            >
   276→              <m.div
   277→                tabIndex={-1}
   278→                role={type === 'tooltip' ? 'tooltip' : 'dialog'}
   279→                className={clsxm(
   280→                  !headless && [
   281→                    'rounded-xl border border-zinc-400/20 p-4 outline-hidden backdrop-blur-lg dark:border-zinc-500/30',
   282→                    'bg-white/80 dark:bg-neutral-900/80',
   283→                  ],
   284→
   285→                  'shadow-perfect relative z-[2]',
   286→
   287→                  type === 'tooltip'
   288→                    ? `max-w-[25rem] break-all rounded-xl px-4 py-2`
   289→                    : '',
   290→
   291→                  popoverClassNames,
   292→                )}
   293→                ref={refs.setFloating}
   294→                initial={{ y: '10px', opacity: 0 }}
   295→                animate={{ y: '0px', opacity: 1 }}
   296→                exit={{
   297→                  y: '10px',
   298→                  opacity: 0,
   299→                  transition: { type: 'tween', duration: 0.2 },
   300→                }}
   301→                transition={microReboundPreset}
   302→                style={{
   303→                  position: strategy,
   304→                  top: y ?? '',
   305→                  left: x ?? '',
   306→                  visibility: isPositioned && x !== null ? 'visible' : 'hidden',
   307→                }}
   308→              >
   309→                <PopoverActionContext value={actionCtxValue}>
   310→                  {props.children}
   311→                </PopoverActionContext>
   312→              </m.div>
   313→            </m.div>
   314→          </RootPortal>
   315→        )}
   316→      </AnimatePresence>
   317→    </>
   318→  )
   319→}
   320→
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the issue. Looking at the screenshot and the code:

1. The `CommentBlockThread` is rendered inside a `FloatPopover` with `headless` mode, and has its own `popoverClassNames` for styling the container
2. The `CommentBoxRoot` inside the thread is showing a full comment box with avatar, input area, and all the bells and whistles, which looks oversized and out of place in a compact inline thread popover

The problems I see from the screenshot:
1. The comment box area is too large and bulky for an inline thread popover
2. The avatar in the comment input area is too prominent


3. The overall layout feels cramped with a full-featured comment box stuffed into a small popover

The real issue is that `CommentBoxRoot` is designed for the full-page comment section and doesn't scale down well for a compact thread context. I need to create a simpler, more minimal input component specifically for inline threads—something closer to what Google Docs or Notion use, with just a text input and no oversized avatar or extra chrome. Looking at the CommentBlockThread popover, I need to examine what CommentBoxAuthedInput and CommentBoxLegacyForm are actually rendering to understand why the UI feels disjointed.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 17:04:20Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a clear picture of the problem. The `CommentBlockThread` uses `CommentBoxRoot` which renders a full-featured comment box - including a 48px avatar, a 150px tall textarea, the action bar, and all the form fields. This is designed for the main page comment section, not a compact inline thread popover of only `w-80` (320px).

From the screenshot I can see:
1. Big avatar (48px) with Google auth badge at bottom
2. 150px textarea with placeholder text
3. "Supports Markdown and GFM" bar at bottom
4. The whole thing barely fits in the 320px wide popover


The current styling with `p-2` and a 60px minimum textarea height doesn't address the real issues - the oversized avatar, the markdown footer text, and the overall proportions that don't work for this constrained space. I need to redesign this for a compact view that strips away unnecessary elements like the large avatar and support text, tightens up the spacing, and creates something that actually fits the popover's dimensions.

I'm weighing two approaches: either build a dedicated compact component for the thread popover, or add a compact mode to the existing CommentBoxRoot. The dedicated component feels cleaner since I can strip it down to just what's needed - a simple text input with a small inline avatar and minimal styling, similar to how Notion or Google Docs handle inline comments. Let me examine what UniversalTextArea and ActionBar are currently rendering to see what I can simplify or remove.
</details></think>

```text
    12→import { useRefValue } from '~/hooks/common/use-ref-value'
    13→import { clsxm } from '~/lib/helper'
    14→import { sample } from '~/lib/lodash'
    15→
    16→import { KAOMOJI_LIST } from '../../dashboard/comments/kaomoji'
    17→import { KaomojiPanel } from '../../shared/KaomojiPanel'
    18→import { getRandomPlaceholder } from './constants'
    19→import {
    20→  useCommentBoxTextValue,
    21→  useSendComment,
    22→  useSetCommentBoxValues,
    23→} from './hooks'
    24→import { CommentBoxSlotPortal } from './providers'
    25→
    26→const EmojiPicker = dynamic(() =>
    27→  import('../../shared/EmojiPicker').then((mod) => mod.EmojiPicker),
    28→)
    29→
    30→export const UniversalTextArea: Component<{ autoFocus?: boolean }> = ({
    31→  className,
    32→  autoFocus,
    33→}) => {
    34→  const t = useTranslations('common')
    35→  const placeholder = useRefValue(() => getRandomPlaceholder(t.raw))
    36→  const setter = useSetCommentBoxValues()
    37→  const value = useCommentBoxTextValue()
    38→  const [sendComment] = useSendComment()
    39→  const isMobile = useIsMobile()
    40→
    41→  const editorRef = useRef<LexicalEditor | null>(null)
    42→  const shouldAutoFocusByHash =
    43→    typeof location !== 'undefined' && location.hash === '#comment'
    44→  const shouldAutoFocus = !!autoFocus || shouldAutoFocusByHash
    45→
    46→  const handleInsertText = useCallback((text: string) => {
    47→    const editor = editorRef.current
    48→    if (!editor) return
    49→    editor.focus()
    50→    editor.update(() => {
    51→      const selection = $getSelection()
    52→      if ($isRangeSelection(selection)) {
    53→        selection.insertText(` ${text} `)
    54→      }
    55→    })
    56→  }, [])
    57→
    58→  const contentClassName = useMemo(
    59→    () => clsxm(className, 'overflow-auto flex-1 h-0! min-h-0!'),
    60→    [className],
    61→  )
    62→
    63→  return (
    64→    <MarkdownEditor
    65→      value={value}
    66→      onChange={(next) => setter('text', next)}
    67→      className="relative flex flex-col overflow-hidden pb-8"
    68→      placeholder={placeholder}
    69→      onSubmit={sendComment}
    70→      autoFocus={shouldAutoFocus}
    71→      scrollIntoView={shouldAutoFocusByHash}
    72→      contentClassName={contentClassName}
    73→      onEditorReady={(editor) => {
    74→        editorRef.current = editor
    75→      }}
    76→      actions={
    77→        <CommentBoxSlotPortal>
    78→          {!isMobile && (
    79→            <FloatPopover
    80→              mobileAsSheet
    81→              trigger="click"
    82→              triggerElement={
    83→                <div
    84→                  className="center ml-0 inline-flex size-5 translate-y-1 text-base md:ml-4"
    85→                  role="button"
    86→                  tabIndex={0}
    87→                >
    88→                  <i className="i-mingcute-emoji-2-line" />
    89→                  <span className="sr-only">{t('emoji_label')}</span>
    90→                </div>
    91→              }
    92→              headless
    93→              popoverWrapperClassNames="z-[999]"
    94→              popoverClassNames="pointer-events-auto"
    95→            >
    96→              <EmojiPicker onEmojiSelect={handleInsertText} />
    97→            </FloatPopover>
    98→          )}
    99→          <KaomojiPanel placement="bottom" onInsert={handleInsertText}>
   100→            <div
   101→              role="button"
   102→              tabIndex={0}
   103→              className="center ml-0 inline-flex shrink-0 text-xs md:ml-4"
   104→            >
   105→              {useMemo(() => sample(KAOMOJI_LIST), [])}
   106→              <span className="sr-only">{t('kaomoji_label')}</span>
   107→            </div>
   108→          </KaomojiPanel>
   109→        </CommentBoxSlotPortal>
   110→      }
   111→    />
   112→  )
   113→}
   114→
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a complete picture. The problem is clear:

The `CommentBlockThread` popover (w-80, 320px wide) renders a full-size `CommentBoxRoot` that includes:
1. A 48px avatar with auth badge
2. A 150px tall textarea (`h-[150px]`)
3. "Supports Markdown and GFM" footer
4. Emoji/kaomoji picker buttons
5. The send button, whisper checkbox, etc.

This is way too much UI for a compact inline thread popover. The override `className="p-2 [&_textarea]:!min-h-[60px]"` doesn't actually help much because the textarea height is set as `h-[150px]` on the container div, not min-h on the textarea itself.
 
I need to add a `compact` prop to `CommentBoxRoot` that hides the avatar, reduces the textarea height, strips out the markdown footer and emoji pickers, and tightens up the padding and action bar to fit the narrow popover space. Actually, looking at the CSS override approach in the thread, I can see the limitations—the avatar size and textarea height are hardcoded in ways that are hard to override with parent selectors, and the spacing is too generous for a compact layout. I think passing a `compact` boolean through context so the inner components can adapt their styles is the cleanest path forward, so let me check how the providers are set up.
</details></think>

```text
     1→'use client'

     4→import { atom } from 'jotai'
     5→import { atomWithStorage } from 'jotai/utils'
     6→import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from 'react'
     7→import { createContext, memo, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react'
     8→
     9→import { useBeforeMounted } from '~/hooks/common/use-before-mounted'
    10→import { jotaiStore } from '~/lib/store'
    11→
    12→import type { CommentAnchor } from '../types'
    13→import { setCommentActionLeftSlot, useCommentActionLeftSlot } from './hooks'
    14→
    15→export const commentStoragePrefix = 'comment-'
    16→export const createInitialValue = () => ({
    17→  refId: atom(''),
    18→
    19→  text: atom(''),
    20→  author: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}author`, ''),
    21→  mail: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}mail`, ''),
    22→  url: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}url`, ''),
    23→
    24→  avatar: atom(''),
    25→  source: atom(''),
    26→
    27→  anchor: atom<CommentAnchor | null>(null),
    28→
    29→  // settings
    30→  isWhisper: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}is-whisper`, false),
    31→  syncToRecently: atomWithStorage(
    32→    `${commentStoragePrefix}sync-to-recently`,
    33→    true,
    34→  ),
    35→})
    36→export const CommentBoxContext = createContext<
    37→  ReturnType<typeof createInitialValue>
    38→>(null!)
    39→
    40→export const CommentBoxLifeCycleContext = createContext<{
    41→  afterSubmit?: () => void
    42→}>(null!)
    43→
    44→export const CommentBoxProvider = (
    45→  props: PropsWithChildren & {
    46→    refId: string
    47→    afterSubmit?: () => void
    48→    initialValue?: string
    49→    anchor?: CommentAnchor | null
    50→  },
    51→) => {
    52→  const { refId, children, afterSubmit, initialValue, anchor } = props
    53→
    54→  const ctxValue = useMemo(
    55→    () => ({
    56→      ...createInitialValue(),
    57→      refId: atom(refId),
    58→    }),
    59→    [refId],
    60→  )
    61→  useBeforeMounted(() => {
    62→    if (initialValue) {
    63→      jotaiStore.set(ctxValue.text, initialValue)
    64→    }
    65→    if (anchor) {
    66→      jotaiStore.set(ctxValue.anchor, anchor)
    67→    }
    68→  })
    69→  return (
    70→    <CommentBoxContext key={refId} value={ctxValue}>
    71→      <CommentBoxLifeCycleContext
    72→        value={useMemo(() => ({ afterSubmit }), [afterSubmit])}
    73→      >
    74→        {children}
    75→      </CommentBoxLifeCycleContext>
    76→    </CommentBoxContext>
    77→  )
    78→}
    79→
    80→export const CommentIsReplyContext = createContext(false)
    81→export const CommentOriginalRefIdContext = createContext('')
    82→export const CommentCompletedCallbackContext = createContext<
    83→  null | ((comment: CommentModel) => void)
    84→>(null)
    85→export const CommentIsReplyProvider = (
    86→  props: PropsWithChildren<{
    87→    isReply: boolean
    88→    originalRefId: string
    89→    onCompleted?: (comment: CommentModel) => void
    90→  }>,
    91→) => {
    92→  const { isReply, originalRefId, onCompleted, children } = props
    93→  return (
    94→    <CommentOriginalRefIdContext value={originalRefId}>
    95→      <CommentIsReplyContext value={isReply}>
    96→        <CommentCompletedCallbackContext value={onCompleted || null}>
    97→          {children}
    98→        </CommentCompletedCallbackContext>
    99→      </CommentIsReplyContext>
   100→    </CommentOriginalRefIdContext>
   101→  )
   102→}
   103→
   104→export const CommentBoxSlotPortal = memo((props: PropsWithChildren) => {
   105→  const { children } = props
   106→  useEffect(() => {
   107→    setCommentActionLeftSlot(children)
   108→    return () => {
   109→      setCommentActionLeftSlot(null)
   110→    }
   111→  }, [children])
   112→  return null
   113→})
   114→
   115→export const CommentBoxSlotProvider: FC = memo(() => useCommentActionLeftSlot())
   116→
   117→CommentBoxSlotProvider.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotProvider'
   118→CommentBoxSlotPortal.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotPortal'
   119→
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 17:05:03Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a clear understanding of the problem and the architecture. Let me think about the best approach to fix this.

The core issue is that the `CommentBoxRoot` is designed for full-page use and doesn't adapt to a compact inline thread context. The popover at 320px wide tries to render:
1. A 48px avatar
2. A 150px tall textarea container
3. Full action bar with "Supports Markdown and GFM" text
4. Emoji/kaomoji pickers

This is way too much for a compact popover.

**My approach**: Add a `compact` prop to `CommentBaseProps` and `CommentBoxRoot`, then create a `CommentCompactContext` that inner components can use to render in compact mode.


In compact mode I'd hide the avatar, shrink the textarea to around 80px, strip out the markdown support text, simplify the action bar, and tighten up spacing. But CSS overrides alone won't cut it since the components have fixed dimensions baked in with Tailwind classes like `size-[48px]` and `h-[150px]`, plus there's too much nested structure to override cleanly. I need to go with the context-based approach to let components conditionally render their compact versions.

I'll add a `compact` boolean to `CommentBaseProps`, create a context to pass it through the component tree, and have the form components read from it to apply the right styling. This means updating the types, adding the context provider, threading it through `CommentBoxRoot` and `CommentBoxProvider`, then reading it in the input, form, and action bar components to toggle compact styles.

Actually, I'm second-guessing whether I need all this infrastructure—maybe I should just use aggressive CSS overrides in `CommentBlockThread` instead, hiding the avatar and reducing the textarea height with class selectors. But that approach feels brittle and hard to maintain.

Let me step back and think about what the user actually wants: they said the UI looks bad and uncoordinated in the inline comment thread. For a popover comment interface, the ideal would be a clean, compact list with small avatars and a simple text input at the bottom—not a full rich text editor. Creating a whole new component seems like overkill though, so I should focus on adapting the existing ones to a compact mode.

Now I'm going to implement this by adding a compact context and adjusting the key components: shrinking avatars to 28px and hiding the auth badge, reducing the textarea height to 80px, tightening spacing, and hiding the markdown support text in compact mode. I'll pass this flag through to the comment thread and clean up any existing overrides.
</details></think>

```text
   110→export const useSendComment = () => {
   111→  const commentRefId = useCommentBoxRefIdValue()
   112→  const {
   113→    text: textAtom,
   114→    author: authorAtom,
   115→    mail: mailAtom,
   116→    url: urlAtom,
   117→
   118→    source: sourceAtom,
   119→    avatar: avatarAtom,
   120→
   121→    anchor: anchorAtom,
   122→
   123→    isWhisper: isWhisperAtom,
   124→    syncToRecently: syncToRecentlyAtom,
   125→  } = useGetCommentBoxAtomValues()
   126→  const { afterSubmit } = useCommentBoxLifeCycle()
   127→  const isLogged = useIsOwnerLogged()
   128→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
   129→  const isReply = useUseCommentReply()
   130→  const originalRefId = useCommentOriginalRefId()
   131→  const completedCallback = useCommentCompletedCallback()
   132→
   133→  const wrappedCompletedCallback = <T extends CommentModel>(data: T): T => {
   134→    completedCallback?.(data)
   135→    return data
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  const { mutate, isPending } = useMutation({
   139→    mutationFn: async (refId: string) => {
   140→      const text = jotaiStore.get(textAtom)
   141→      const author = jotaiStore.get(authorAtom)
   142→      const mail = jotaiStore.get(mailAtom)
   143→      const avatar = jotaiStore.get(avatarAtom)
   144→      const source = jotaiStore.get(sourceAtom) as any
   145→      const url = jotaiStore.get(urlAtom)
   146→      const anchor = jotaiStore.get(anchorAtom)
   147→
   148→      const commentDto: CommentDto & { anchor?: CommentAnchor } = { text, author, mail, avatar, source, url }
   149→      if (anchor) commentDto.anchor = anchor
   150→
   151→      if (isLogged) {
   152→        delete commentDto.avatar
   153→      }
   154→
   155→      // Omit empty string key
   156→      Object.keys(commentDto).forEach((key) => {
   157→        // @ts-expect-error
   158→        if (commentDto[key] === '') delete commentDto[key]
   159→      })
   160→
   161→      // Reply Comment
   162→      if (isReply) {
   163→        if (isLogged) {
   164→          return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
   165→            .reply(refId)
   166→            .post<CommentModel>({
   167→              data: {
   168→                text,
   169→                source,
   170→              },
   171→            })
   172→            .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   173→        } else {
   174→          return apiClient.comment
   175→            .reply(refId, commentDto)
   176→            .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   177→        }
   178→      }
   179→
   180→      // Normal Comment
   181→      const isWhisper = jotaiStore.get(isWhisperAtom)
   182→      const syncToRecently = jotaiStore.get(syncToRecentlyAtom)
   183→
   184→      if (isLogged) {
   185→        return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
   186→          .comment(refId)
   187→          .post<CommentModel>({
   188→            data: { text, source, ...(anchor ? { anchor } : {}) },
   189→          })
   190→          .then(async (res) => {
   191→            if (syncToRecently)
   192→              apiClient.recently.proxy
   193→                .post({
   194→                  data: {
   195→                    content: text,
   196→                    ref: refId,
   197→                  },
   198→                })
   199→                .then(() => {
   200→                  toast.success('已同步到碎碎念')
   201→                })
   202→
   203→            return res
   204→          })
   205→          .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   206→      }
   207→      // @ts-ignore
   208→      commentDto.isWhispers = isWhisper
   209→      return apiClient.comment
   210→        .comment(refId, commentDto)
   211→        .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   212→    },
   213→    mutationKey: [commentRefId, 'comment'],
   214→    onError(error: RequestError) {
   215→      toast.error(getErrorMessageFromRequestError(error))
   216→    },
   217→    onSuccess(data) {
   218→      afterSubmit?.()
   219→
   220→      const toastCopy = isLogged
   221→        ? '发表成功啦~'
   222→        : isReply
   223→          ? '感谢你的回复！'
   224→          : '感谢你的评论！'
   225→
   226→      const commentListQueryKey = buildCommentsQueryKey(originalRefId)
   227→
   228→      toast.success(toastCopy)
   229→      jotaiStore.set(textAtom, '')
   230→      jotaiStore.set(anchorAtom, null)
   231→
   232→      queryClient.setQueryData<
   233→        InfiniteData<
   234→          PaginateResult<
   235→            CommentModel & {
   236→              ref: string
   237→            }
   238→          >
   239→        >
   240→      >(commentListQueryKey, (oldData) => {
   241→        if (!oldData) return oldData
   242→        if (isReply) {
   243→          // find the reply refed comment
   244→
   245→          return produce(oldData, (draft) => {
   246→            const dfs = (
   247→              data: CommentModel,
   248→              commentRefId: string,
   249→              newData: CommentModel & { new?: boolean },
   250→            ) => {
   251→              if (data.id === commentRefId) {
   252→                if (!data.children) {
   253→                  data.children = []
   254→                }
   255→                ;(data.children as (CommentModel & { new: boolean })[]).push({
   256→                  ...newData,
   257→                  new: true,
   258→                })
   259→                return true
   260→              }
   261→              if (!data.children) {
   262→                return
   263→              }
   264→              for (const child of data.children) {
   265→                if (dfs(child, commentRefId, newData)) {
   266→                  return true
   267→                }
   268→              }
   269→              return false
   270→            }
   271→
   272→            const dataToAdd = {
   273→              ...data,
   274→              new: true,
   275→            }
   276→
   277→            for (const page of draft.pages) {
   278→              for (const item of page.data) {
   279→                if (dfs(item, commentRefId, dataToAdd)) {
   280→                  break
   281→                }
   282→              }
   283→            }
   284→          })
   285→        }
   286→
   287→        return produce(oldData, (draft) => {
   288→          draft.pages[0].data.unshift({
   289→            ...data,
   290→            // @ts-ignore
   291→            new: true,
   292→          })
   293→        })
   294→      })
   295→    },
   296→  })
   297→
   298→  return [
   299→    useCallback(() => mutate(commentRefId), [commentRefId, mutate]),
   300→    isPending,
   301→  ] as const
   302→}
   303→
```
</details></tool-use>

Now I have a full understanding. Let me implement the compact mode. Here's my plan:

I'm reconsidering whether a context is the right approach versus using CSS overrides instead. Context is cleaner architecturally but requires threading it through multiple files, whereas CSS overrides from the parent would be more localized. I think I'll go with a hybrid approach using targeted CSS overrides in `CommentBlockThread` to keep the changes minimal.

The main issues to address are the avatar size (48px with ring), textarea height (150px), the markdown support text, and overall spacing. Actually, I'm leaning toward the context approach since it's more maintainable long-term. But let me reconsider what the user actually needs—they said the UI looks "uncoordinated," so maybe aggressive CSS overrides via Tailwind classes on the wrapper would be the fastest way to make the thread popover look better and keep everything localized.

Refining the CSS overrides I'd need...

Actually, this is getting too fragile with selectors. I'm going to implement a context-based solution instead. I'll add a `CommentCompactContext` to providers, update the types to include a compact flag, wrap the Root component with the provider, create a hook to access it, then use that hook across AuthedInput, CommentBoxLegacyForm, ActionBar, and CommentBlockThread to apply the compact styling where needed.

Setting up the context infrastructure...

```text
    64→
```

1. **`types.ts`** — `CommentBaseProps` 增加 `compact?: boolean`
2. **`providers.tsx`** — 新增 `CommentCompactContext`
3. **`hooks.tsx`** — 新增 `useCommentCompact` hook
4. **`Root.tsx`** — 用 `CommentCompactContext` 包裹子组件，compact 模式隐藏 `SwitchCommentMode`
5. **`AuthedInput.tsx`** — compact 模式：24px 头像（无 auth 徽标），80px 文本域，紧凑间距
6. **`CommentBoxLegacyForm.tsx`** — compact 模式：`LoggedForm` 同上，`FormWithUserInfo` 隐藏姓名/邮箱/URL 表单行
7. **`ActionBar.tsx`** — compact 模式隐藏 "Supports Markdown and GFM" 文字
8. **`CommentBlockThread.tsx`** — 传入 `compact`，移除 `[&_textarea]:!min-h-[60px]` hack

核心效果：弹窗内评论输入区从全尺寸（48px 头像 + 150px 文本域 + Markdown 提示）变为紧凑版（24px 头像 + 80px 文本域，无多余 chrome），与 320px 弹窗协调。

I'm seeing the avatar taking up unnecessary space, the textarea has too much visual weight with its background color, and the emoji row feels out of place.     55→}

```text
    61→  onClose,
    62→}: CommentBlockThreadProps) {

    64→
```

```text
    24→import { CommentBoxSlotPortal } from './providers'

    30→export const UniversalTextArea: Component<{ autoFocus?: boolean }> = ({
    31→  className,
    32→  autoFocus,
    33→}) => {
    34→  const t = useTranslations('common')
    35→  const placeholder = useRefValue(() => getRandomPlaceholder(t.raw))
    36→  const setter = useSetCommentBoxValues()

    38→  const [sendComment] = useSendComment()
    39→  const isMobile = useIsMobile()

    46→  const handleInsertText = useCallback((text: string) => {

    49→    editor.focus()

    51→      const selection = $getSelection()
    52→      if ($isRangeSelection(selection)) {
    53→        selection.insertText(` ${text} `)
    54→      }
    55→    })
    56→  }, [])

    58→  const contentClassName = useMemo(

    60→    [className],
    61→  )
    62→
    63→  return (
    64→    <MarkdownEditor
    65→      value={value}
    66→      onChange={(next) => setter('text', next)}
    67→      className="relative flex flex-col overflow-hidden pb-8"
    68→      placeholder={placeholder}
    69→      onSubmit={sendComment}
    70→      autoFocus={shouldAutoFocus}
    71→      scrollIntoView={shouldAutoFocusByHash}
    72→      contentClassName={contentClassName}
    73→      onEditorReady={(editor) => {
    74→        editorRef.current = editor
    75→      }}
    76→      actions={

    86→                  tabIndex={0}

    89→                  <span className="sr-only">{t('emoji_label')}</span>
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I can see the full picture. Looking at the screenshot again:

Issues:
1. `w-80` (320px) is too narrow - feels cramped
2. The comment input area still has avatar + textarea + action bar which looks cluttered in the small space
3. The emoji and kaomoji buttons at the bottom look awkward
4. The overall look is "not generous" - needs more breathing room

For a "大气" (generous, spacious) redesign:

1. **Wider popover**: `w-[420px]` or similar
2. **Completely remove avatar from input area in compact mode** - it's redundant since the user is already identified


3. **Hide emoji/kaomoji buttons in compact mode** - they clutter the view without adding much value
4. **Simplify the input**: Just a clean textarea with better spacing and no unnecessary elements
5. **Reduce visual noise**: The textarea background and nested boxes create too many layers

I need to check how the MarkdownEditor's `actions` slot works and examine the textarea rendering to understand what I'm working with. The core issues are the narrow width, the redundant avatar taking up space, the emoji row wasting real estate, and too many visual containers stacked together.

I'm going to redesign this more aggressively: widen the comment block, strip out the avatar from compact inputs, hide the emoji/kaomoji actions, and simplify the textarea styling so it doesn't create visual clutter with nested backgrounds. In compact mode, the textarea should be more subtle—maybe just relying on the popover's container instead of adding another background layer.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is saying that autoFocus doesn't work in the compact comment thread popover, and suggests using ref + effect to focus the editor. Let me look at how the MarkdownEditor handles focus to understand the issue.

The problem is likely that when the FloatPopover opens and renders the CommentBlockThread, the autoFocus on the textarea/editor doesn't fire because the component is rendered in a portal after the initial mount. The standard autoFocus attribute only works on initial DOM mount, but portaled content may not trigger it properly.

Let me look at the MarkdownEditor to understand how autoFocus is handled there, and then figure out where to add the ref + effect focus logic.


I see now—the CommentBoxRoot component already supports an autoFocus prop, but it's not being passed when rendering the thread. I need to add autoFocus to the CommentBlockThread's CommentBoxRoot component to enable focus when the popover opens. I'm tracing how `autoFocus` flows through the component hierarchy from `CommentBoxRoot` down to `MarkdownEditor`, and I see it also checks the URL hash as a fallback. The most practical solution is to add a ref to the container in `CommentBlockThread` and use an effect to programmatically focus the editor element after the popover mounts, since the timing of portal rendering can interfere with the standard `autoFocus` prop.
</details></think>

```text
     1→'use client'

     3→import type { CommentModel } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     4→import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
     5→import { useCallback } from 'react'
     6→
     7→import { Avatar } from '~/components/ui/avatar'
     8→import { RelativeTime } from '~/components/ui/relative-time'
     9→
    10→import { CommentBoxRoot } from './CommentBox/Root'
    11→import type { CommentAnchor } from './types'
    12→
    13→type CommentWithAnchor = CommentModel & { anchor?: CommentAnchor }
    14→
    15→interface CommentBlockThreadProps {
    16→  comments: CommentWithAnchor[]
    17→  refId: string
    18→  anchor: CommentAnchor
    19→  onClose?: () => void
    20→}
    21→
    22→function ThreadComment({ comment }: { comment: CommentWithAnchor }) {
    23→  const { avatar, author } = comment
    24→
    25→  return (
    26→    <div className="flex gap-2 px-3 py-2">
    27→      <Avatar
    28→        shadow={false}
    29→        imageUrl={avatar}
    30→        text={author}
    31→        size={24}
    32→        radius="full"
    33→        className="shrink-0 rounded-full"
    34→      />
    35→      <div className="min-w-0 flex-1">
    36→        <div className="flex items-baseline gap-1.5">
    37→          <span className="text-[13px] font-semibold text-foreground/90">
    38→            {author}
    39→          </span>
    40→          <span className="text-[11px] text-muted-foreground">
    41→            <RelativeTime date={comment.created} />
    42→          </span>
    43→        </div>
    44→        {comment.anchor?.mode === 'range' && (
    45→          <div className="mt-0.5 border-l-2 border-accent/40 pl-2 text-xs italic text-muted-foreground">
    46→            {comment.anchor.quote}
    47→          </div>
    48→        )}
    49→        <p className="mt-0.5 text-[13px] leading-relaxed text-foreground/80">
    50→          {comment.text}
    51→        </p>
    52→      </div>
    53→    </div>
    54→  )
    55→}
    56→
    57→export function CommentBlockThread({
    58→  comments,
    59→  refId,
    60→  anchor,
    61→  onClose,
    62→}: CommentBlockThreadProps) {
    63→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    64→
    65→  const handleAfterSubmit = useCallback(() => {
    66→    queryClient.invalidateQueries({
    67→      queryKey: ['comments', refId, 'anchors'],
    68→    })
    69→    onClose?.()
    70→  }, [queryClient, refId, onClose])
    71→
    72→  return (
    73→    <div className="w-[400px] min-w-0 overflow-hidden">
    74→      {comments.length > 0 && (
    75→        <div className="max-h-80 overflow-y-auto py-1">
    76→          {comments.map((comment) => (
    77→            <ThreadComment key={comment.id} comment={comment} />
    78→          ))}
    79→        </div>
    80→      )}
    81→      <div className={comments.length > 0 ? 'border-t border-border/50' : ''}>
    82→        <CommentBoxRoot
    83→          refId={refId}
    84→          anchor={anchor}
    85→          afterSubmit={handleAfterSubmit}
    86→          compact
    87→          className="px-3 py-2.5"
    88→        />
    89→      </div>
    90→    </div>
    91→  )
    92→}
    93→
```
